stork finds singing at death unusual
swan explains she goes to a place free from traps and hunger
swan welcomes death as liberation
death framed as freedom not loss

title: A Wench parting with her Sweetheart
narrative_elements: girl is distressed after parting from sweetheart
neighbor thinks she weeps for love
girl reveals she cries because he took his coat
she expected the coat as part of his gifts
true grief is about material loss not emotional loss

title: A Fly upon a Wheel
narrative_elements: fly rides on coach wheel
fly boasts about the dust it raises
fly climbs onto horse's back
fly boasts about its speed
fly takes credit for others' work

title: An Eele and a Snake
narrative_elements: eel notes similarity to snake
eel puzzled why hunters fear snake but not eel
snake explains it defends itself when harmed
eel lacks defensive capability
fear is earned through ability to retaliate

title: The monkey, donkey and mole
narrative_elements: donkey complains about lacking horns
monkey complains about lacking a tail
mole silences both
mole points out it lacks eyes entirely
others' hardship greater than one's own complaint

title: Seamen Praying to Saints
narrative_elements: terrible storm at sea
each sailor prays to different saint
wise sailor warns messengers may not reach in time
sailors redirect prayers directly to God
storm calms after unified prayer

title: The Fishes and the Frying-Pan
narrative_elements: cook fries live fish in pan
fish feel heat and cry out
fish jump from pan into fire
situation becomes far worse
rash escape leads to worse fate

title: A League of Beasts and Fishes
narrative_elements: beasts and fishes form alliance against birds
war is declared
fishes send excuse instead of forces
fishes claim they cannot travel by land
alliance fails due to impractical commitment

title: A Covetous Ambassador
narrative_elements: ambassador annoyed by musicians demanding payment
ambassador claims to be in mourning to avoid paying
nobleman visits to offer condolences
nobleman asks how long mother has been dead
ambassador admits it has been forty years, exposing the lie

title: An Old Friend and a Cardinal
narrative_elements: old friend promoted to Cardinal
gentleman visits to congratulate him
Cardinal fails to recognize old friend
gentleman delivers biting critique of high rank
high status causes forgetfulness and blindness

title: A Young Droll and a Crooked Old Man
narrative_elements: young man mocks bent old man
young man asks to buy his bow
old man rebukes the fool calmly
old man predicts same fate awaits the youth
age brings understanding of what was mocked

title: An Old Fellow and a Young Wench
narrative_elements: old man lives to 70 without knowing women
devil tricks him into marrying a young girl
man complains of mismatched desires
young wife wants a husband but old man cannot satisfy
mismatched timing of desire leads to regret

title: An Eagle and a Pye
narrative_elements: magpie believes birds need better leadership
magpie asks eagle to join council
magpie boasts of appearance and ability to make noise
eagle acknowledges magpie's talkativeness
eagle refuses because magpie chatters too much for leadership

title: Town-Dogs and Country-Dogs
narrative_elements: stray dog is chased and bitten by many town dogs
dog turns and faces chasers
chasers back down when confronted
captain uses the example to instruct soldiers
those who stand and fight are safer than those who flee

title: An Old Woman and the Devil
narrative_elements: devil anticipates old woman will climb tree and fall
devil summons witnesses to preempt blame
old woman falls from tree as predicted
old woman blames devil as expected
devil presents witnesses to prove her own culpability

title: The Frogs and Tortoises
narrative_elements: frogs play and jump in meadow
tortoises envy frogs' liveliness
frogs are caught and eaten by birds and fish
wise tortoise observes the danger
slow and steady living is safer than quick and lively

title: The Mice and the Oak
narrative_elements: mice find it troublesome to climb oak for acorns
mice decide to gnaw tree down
wise old mouse warns against destroying their provider
asks who will feed them if tree is gone
short-term convenience vs long-term survival

title: A Run-away Dog and his Master
narrative_elements: dog runs away from master
stranger finds dog and returns him
master questions dog's ingratitude
master claims he never beat dog
dog explains that punishment by master's order is same as master's own hand

title: The Birds and Beetles
narrative_elements: birds panic fearing beetles' dirt balls as bullets
wise sparrow calms the birds
sparrow points out beetles can barely roll pellets on ground
beetles' threat cannot reach birds in the air
fear overcome by rational observation

title: A Bear and Bees
narrative_elements: bear is stung by single bee
bear in rage attacks entire bee garden
bear knocks over all hives in revenge
swarms of bees attack bear
bear realizes he should have ignored one sting

title: A Fowler and a Chaffinch
narrative_elements: fowler sets net and bait
birds come and go all day in small numbers
fowler waits hoping for bigger catch
evening comes and birds sleep
fowler catches only one late chaffinch having missed many opportunities

title: A Soldier and two Horses
narrative_elements: soldier has excellent first horse
soldier buys inferior second horse
soldier favors and cares more for new horse
others wonder at preferring worse horse
observation: attraction to the newest arrival is natural

title: A Spaniel and a Sow
narrative_elements: sow questions how spaniel is loyal despite blows
spaniel considers good food and kind words too
spaniel weighs treatment overall not just negative
loyal despite hardship because benefits outweigh costs
balanced assessment of relationship

title: Oxen and Timber
narrative_elements: timber in cart urges oxen to hurry
timber says load is not heavy
oxen reveal timber's grim fate as beams and supports
oxen will be free of load at journey's end
timber must bear weight until it breaks

title: A Goldfinch and a Boy
narrative_elements: goldfinch escapes from master's cage
boy tries to lure bird back
boy promises bird won't be as well cared for elsewhere
bird prefers self-care over others' care
freedom valued over comfort and security

title: A Lapwing Preferr'd
narrative_elements: all birds invited to eagle's wedding
hoopoe given higher seat of honor
birds resent hoopoe's preferential treatment
hoopoe notable for crown and colorful feathers
hoopoe spends time in dung and filth despite fine appearance

title: A Priest and Pears
narrative_elements: gluttonous priest finds pears on journey
priest disdains pears and defiles them
river swollen with rain blocks further journey
priest forced to turn back starving
priest must eat the pears he defiled to survive

title: A Horse and a Hog
narrative_elements: hog calls horse a fool for charging into battle
hog warns horse of destruction ahead
horse retorts that hog is fattened only to be killed
horse claims to leave behind a good name
honor in brave death vs ignominious slaughter

title: A Hunts-man and a Currier
narrative_elements: tanner buys bearskin before bear is caught
hunter claims he will kill bear tomorrow
bear overpowers hunter
hunter plays dead to survive
tanner reveals bear whispered to catch bear before selling skin

title: A Hermit and a Soldier
narrative_elements: holy man warns soldier about dangers and sins of war
soldier agrees to give up soldiering
soldier's reason is poor pay and little plunder
soldier turns to religion only when earthly gains fail
piety motivated by worldly failure not genuine virtue

title: A Husband and Wife twice Married
narrative_elements: husband and wife each boast about late spouses
wife gives beggar food in name of late husband
husband gives beggar remaining food in name of late wife
both go without dinner in their spite
mutual spite causes self-harm

title: A Lion and a Mouse
narrative_elements: lion trapped in net
mouse frees lion in exchange for daughter's hand
lion agrees out of generosity
mouse bride accidentally steps on husband
act of kindness leads to tragic outcome

title: Wax and Brick
narrative_elements: debate about wax's fragility vs brick's strength
wax concludes fire made brick hard
wax throws itself into fire hoping to harden
fire melts wax instead of hardening it
what strengthens one destroys another

title: A Husbandman turn'd Soldier and Merchant
narrative_elements: farmer complains of hard agricultural life
farmer becomes soldier hoping for honor and riches
soldier loses limb in battle and returns home
farmer becomes merchant and loads a ship
ship wrecks in storm losing everything

title: An Ass puts in for an Office
narrative_elements: buffoon given office for making loud sounds with gut
donkey demands same recognition
donkey claims to emit even louder and odorless farts
donkey presents this as superior qualification
absurd basis for public office exposed

title: A River and a Fountain
narrative_elements: river boasts about fish, navigation and grand towns
river mocks fountain for obscure existence
fountain is offended and stops its spring
river's channel dries up from lost supply
fish die and river is ruined by its own arrogance

title: A Wicked Man and the Devil
narrative_elements: criminal makes friend of devil to escape troubles
devil helps criminal out of many jails
criminal keeps getting caught and calling on devil
devil finally arrives with worn-out shoes
devil declares he has exhausted himself in criminal's service and abandons him

title: A Council of Birds for Chusing more Kings
narrative_elements: birds deliberate on adding more kings
eagle alone cannot rule so many flocks
crow warns against plan
crow explains more kings means more sacks to fill
one corrupt ruler is bad enough; many would be worse

title: A Woman that would needs Die for her Husband
narrative_elements: woman panics about losing sick husband
woman prays for death to take her instead
death appears beside woman
woman politely redirects death to husband in bed
offer of self-sacrifice quickly abandoned when death actually arrives

title: A Son Singing at his Mother's Funeral
narrative_elements: father weeps following wife's coffin
son follows singing cheerfully
father rebukes son for singing at funeral
son notes father pays priests to sing
father explains roles: it is priests' job to sing, son's duty to cry

title: A Man that would not take a Clyster
narrative_elements: rich man is ill and many doctors attend
doctor prescribes enema for cure
patient doesn't know what an enema is
patient rages at being told to treat his backside when his head hurts
ignorance and pride prevent treatment

title: A Wolf and a Sick Ass
narrative_elements: donkey is ill and near death
wolves and dogs come to inquire after him
son answers through a crack in the door
son replies the donkey is better than they wish
shrewd answer reveals awareness of predators' true intent

title: The Ass's Wish
narrative_elements: donkey suffers in winter and yearns for spring
spring brings harder labor from potter master
donkey longs for summer hoping for rest during harvest
summer brings yet more carrying
each season brings new burdens; no rest comes

title: A Cat and Mice
narrative_elements: mice peek out to survey surroundings
cat sits calm and harmless on shelf
foolish mouse decides cat looks friendly
mouse approaches cat out of curiosity
cat tears mouse to pieces upon contact

title: A Wolf and a Porcupine
narrative_elements: wolf tries to persuade porcupine to disarm
wolf claims it is peacetime and quills look bad
wolf offers porcupine can pick up quills when needed
porcupine rejects the argument
porcupine says while wolf is near it is always war

title: A Mouse and a Kite
narrative_elements: kite gets caught in a net
mouse gnaws hole in net to free kite
kite promises nothing but freedom in thanks
kite immediately eats mouse after being freed
good deed punished with betrayal and death

title: A Cockle and Jupiter
narrative_elements: cockle asks Jupiter to unite house and body
Jupiter says it would be a burden not a favor
cockle accepts the burden
cockle prefers burden to exposure to bad neighbors
security worth the weight of carrying it

title: A Hedge-Hog and a Snake
narrative_elements: snake lets hedgehog into den during cold winter
hedgehog's spines make snake uncomfortable
snake tells hedgehog to find another place
hedgehog refuses and says snake should leave if uncomfortable
the one who offered hospitality is pushed out

title: A Fox and a Hare
narrative_elements: fox and hare debate which manages better in world
hare boasts of swift legs outrunning dogs
fox boasts of outwitting more with cunning than hare with speed
each has a different survival strategy
debate ends with no clear winner

title: An Old Man resolv'd to give over Whoring
narrative_elements: old man still harbors lustful thoughts despite frailty
spiritual advisor counsels against desires of flesh
old man thanks advisor for counsel
old man admits his body is no longer fit for such activities
repentance driven by incapacity not genuine virtue

title: An Impertinent and a Philosopher
narrative_elements: impertinent fellow visits philosopher
philosopher is alone in study
visitor wonders how philosopher can bear solitude
philosopher says he was in good company until visitor arrived
solitude as superior to unwanted company

title: A Wolfe in a Sheeps-Skin
narrative_elements: wolf dresses in sheepskin to deceive
wolf attacks lambs under disguise
shepherd catches wolf and hangs it on gallows
neighbors ask why he hangs a sheep
shepherd reveals wolf's nature hidden under sheep's skin

title: A Widow and a Green Ass
narrative_elements: widow wants second husband but fears public mockery
friend tells her to paint donkey green and parade it
green donkey causes excitement for first few days
excitement dies down quickly as novelty fades
friend explains a widow remarrying is just like a green donkey

title: A Sheep-Biter Hang'd
narrative_elements: shepherd's favorite dog is secretly stealing sheep
shepherd discovers trusted dog is the thief
dog begs mercy as member of household
dog deflects blame to wolves
shepherd says betrayal by trusted dog is far worse than open wolf attacks

title: A Bull and a Ram
narrative_elements: ram defeats all other rams and grows proud
ram challenges bull to a fight
bull knocks ram out at first clash
ram lies as if dead
ram reflects on his arrogance in challenging nature's superior

title: An Incorrigible Son
narrative_elements: father urges son to abandon vicious ways
father speaks at length about virtue
son says father wastes his breath
son has heard many preachers urge virtue more eloquently
son has never taken any of their advice either

title: An Eagle and Rabbets
narrative_elements: eagle takes nest of rabbits and ignores mother's pleas
mother rabbit gathers all rabbits for revenge
rabbits undermine roots of eagle's tree
wind topples tree killing eagles
organized collective action achieves justice against powerful oppressor

title: A Pike sets up for Sovereignty
narrative_elements: pike is prince of river and admired
pike wants to rule the sea too
pike ventures into ocean claiming it as his own
mighty dolphin chases pike back to his stream
pike wisely stays within his own territory thereafter

title: A Sheep picks a Quarrel with a Shepherd
narrative_elements: sheep complains shepherd takes milk and wool
shepherd kills a lamb in anger
sheep asks if that is the worst he can do
shepherd warns he can also cut sheep's throat if he wishes
sheep falls silent fearing worse consequences

title: A Creaking Wheel
narrative_elements: coachman hears one wheel creak
coachman is surprised
coachman notices it is the worst wheel of the set
wheel has little reason to make such noise
those who complain most loudly often deserve it least

title: A Man had a mind to try his Friends
narrative_elements: rich man has many guests due to his open table
rich man suspects some are true friends others freeloaders
at full table he mentions upcoming quarrel needing support
everyone makes excuses except two
two who stayed are counted as true friends, rest are hangers-on

title: A Fox Praising Hare's Flesh
narrative_elements: dog is chasing fox
fox praises hare's flesh to redirect dog
dog abandons fox to chase hare
hare escapes due to speed
hare confronts fox; fox argues praise is better than blame

title: About the hare seeking cunning and the fox seeking swiftness from Jupiter
narrative_elements: fox asks Jupiter for swiftness in addition to slyness
rabbit asks Jupiter for slyness in addition to swiftness
Jupiter refuses both requests
Jupiter explains gifts were distributed generously at creation
giving all gifts to one animal would harm others

title: A Plain Horse wins the Prize
narrative_elements: horses brought to race adorned except one plain horse
other horses mock plain horse as unsuitable
trumpet signals race to begin
plain horse outruns all others easily
plain horse wins the prize; mocked for appearance but victorious in substance

title: A Country-man and a Kid
narrative_elements: countryman in lawsuit seeks lawyer friend repeatedly
lawyer is always too busy to see him
countryman brings a fat kid goat as gift
clerk hears goat and lets man in immediately
man thanks both lawyer and goat for getting him in

title: A Weak Young Man and a Wolfe
narrative_elements: young man marries beautiful young wife
wife's appetite exhausts young man physically
man can barely walk and sits in sun to recuperate
hunters pass by unable to catch swift wolf
man jokes that wife would have quickly stopped wolf's running

title: A Lad Robbing an Orchard
narrative_elements: old man catches boy stealing apples
old man first uses words to stop him
boy ignores warnings
old man tries throwing grass claiming herbs have power
finally throws stones which actually work

title: A Nightingale and a Hawk
narrative_elements: hawk swoops and catches nightingale from branch
nightingale pleads she is too small to be worth eating
nightingale offers to sing for her life
hawk refuses, saying he needs food not music
hawk prioritizes practical need over art and beauty

title: A Lion and a Hog
narrative_elements: lion decides to choose a friend from beasts
all beasts vie to be the favorite
lion chooses hog as companion
lion praises hog's fidelity and steadfastness
true friendship based on loyalty not appearance

title: A Gnat and a Bee
narrative_elements: gnat is frozen and hungry in cold morning
gnat comes to bee-hive asking for shelter and food
gnat offers to teach music in exchange
bee declines politely
bee explains she teaches children her own trade so they can earn by hard work

title: A Lion, Ass, and Hare
narrative_elements: war breaks out between birds and beasts
lion calls all subjects including asses and hares
leaders want to dismiss them as unfit
lion defends keeping them
lion assigns asses as trumpeters and hares as letter-carriers

title: Pigeons Reconcile the Hawks
narrative_elements: hawks are in fierce civil war
pigeons send representatives to make peace
while hawks fight they ignore pigeons
hawks make peace and return to attacking pigeons
pigeons realize their mistake in helping a shared enemy

title: An Old Man that was willing to put off Death
narrative_elements: death visits old man and calls him to come
old man tries to delay claiming short notice
man claims he has never seen death before
death rebukes him listing all signs of mortality
death declares final hour has come with no reprieve

title: A Miser and his Bags
narrative_elements: miser is dying and calls for his bag of ill-gotten money
miser asks who the money will please
bag answers it will please heirs who will squander it
bag says it will please demons who will torment miser's soul eternally
greed brings no reward even in death

title: The Elm and the Willow
narrative_elements: elm mocks willow for bending with every surge of water
elm boasts of its own stoutness and strength
elm claims it has withstood years of persistent stream
violent waves snap elm and drag it away
willow laughs and asks where elm's stoutness is now
